findings.search_query: George Grass DeGrasse wife Eunice Kinney Greenfield Carleton New Brunswick
findings.source_title: George Grass (1819 - 1887) - Genealogy (Geni)
findings.snippet: George Grass (also known as George DeGrasse Sr.) profile lists 'Eunice Bernice DeGrasse Sr. (born Kinney)' in connection with the Kinney family of Carleton/New Brunswick.
findings.claimed_facts: Eunice Bernice Kinney associated as DeGrasse/Grass family member; ties the Kinney surname to the Grass/DeGrasse line in NB. Profile shown is for a George Grass b.1819, likely a descendant, not necessarily her husband.
findings.notes: Web-tree match only. The Geni George Grass shown (b.1819) is too late to be her husband (she d.1832); likely a relative. Could not independently confirm Eunice's own dates/marriage. Needs human review against the specific George Grass who married her.

findings.search_query: Lydia Rachel Fuller 1765 Kingston Massachusetts Simon Knowles Northport Maine findagrave
findings.source_title: Lydia Rachel Fuller Knowles (1766-1857) - Find a Grave Memorial
findings.snippet: Lydia Rachel Fuller Knowles, christened 14 Dec 1766 Kingston, Plymouth Co., MA; daughter of Consider Fuller and Lydia Bryant; died 31 Mar 1857 (age 90y 5m); buried Pitcher Cemetery, Northport, Waldo Co., Maine. Simon B. Knowles (1759-1834) also buried Pitcher Cemetery, Northport.
findings.claimed_facts: Christened 14 Dec 1766 Kingston MA; parents Consider Fuller & Lydia Bryant; died 31 Mar 1857; buried Pitcher Cemetery, Northport, Waldo, ME; husband Simon B. Knowles (1759-1834).
findings.notes: Grave + parents + spouse all match worklist EXCEPT death YEAR: worklist says 1851, Find a Grave/Geni say 31 Mar 1857 (age 90y5m, consistent with a 1766 birth). CONFLICT on death year — flag for review. Also christening 1766 vs worklist birth 'Sep 1765'.

findings.search_query: Abigail Dame 1758 Scarborough Maine Reuben Wescott Gorham; History of Gorham Maine
findings.source_title: The History of Gorham, Maine (Whitney Research Group archive)
findings.snippet: Reuben Wescott married Abigail Dam(e); their children included John Wescott, b. 25 Apr 1786, who m. Martha Whitney 29 Nov 1809 and moved to Standish. Family seated in the Gorham/Scarborough, Cumberland Co., Maine area in the late 1700s.
findings.claimed_facts: Abigail Dam(e) was wife of Reuben Wescott; family in Gorham/Scarborough, Cumberland Co., ME; son John b.1786. Confirms spouse and locale from worklist.
findings.notes: Published town-history match confirms marriage to Reuben Wescott and Gorham/Scarborough locale. Did not find her exact 1758 birth or 1843 death in a primary record; surname spelled Dam/Dame interchangeably.

findings.search_query: Archibald Anderson Warren Maine Scottish weaver Elizabeth Wyllie 1753 Knox
findings.source_title: Surnames A-C — Maine Ulster-Scots Project
findings.snippet: Archibald Anderson was born in Bannockburn, Scotland on April 8, 1718. He was a weaver. He married Ann Malcolm of Glasgow, Scotland. He came to Warren with the Scottish colony in 1753. Archibald Jr. was born in 1753. He married Elizabeth Wylie of Cushing.
findings.claimed_facts: Subject = Archibald Anderson Jr., b. 1753, married Elizabeth Wylie of Cushing; father Archibald Anderson Sr. (b.1718 Bannockburn, weaver) and mother Ann Malcolm of Glasgow; family settled Warren, Knox Co., ME 1753.
findings.notes: Strong match on parents (Archibald Sr. + Ann Malcom), wife (Elizabeth Wyllie), birth year 1753, and Warren ME. CONFLICT: worklist gives birthplace as Glasgow, Scotland; this source has the Scottish colony arriving Warren in 1753 (and the Glasgow tie is the mother's origin), so Archibald Jr. born 1753 may have been born in Warren/Cushing ME, not Glasgow. Flag birthplace for review.

findings.search_query: Calvin Sherman Sharman 1776 Marshfield Robbinston Maine Margaret Towers
findings.source_title: Calvin Sherman (b.1776 Marshfield) / Stephen Sherman (WikiTree)
findings.snippet: Stephen Sherman profile: 'Brother of Robert Sherman and Calvin Sherman (brother b. 1776 Marshfield, Plymouth, Province of Massachusetts Bay).' 'Stephen and his brother Calvin moved to St. Stephen, New Brunswick sometime before October 22, 1800.' A son John D. Sharman had moved to Robbinston, Maine before 1831.
findings.claimed_facts: Calvin Sherman b. 1776 Marshfield, Plymouth Co., MA; moved to St. Stephen, NB before Oct 1800; family later associated with Robbinston, ME. WikiTree profile Sherman-7408 exists for Calvin.
findings.notes: Web-tree match confirms b.1776 Marshfield and the St. Stephen NB / Robbinston ME migration from worklist. Did not verify wife Margaret Towers or 1848 death in a record. Surname appears as both Sherman and Sharman.

findings.search_query: Hannah Doty Doten Washburn 1767 Liverpool Edward Doten Phebe Finney wikitree
findings.source_title: DOTEN Genealogy / Edward Doty family (WikiTree & Doty Family in America)
findings.snippet: Hannah (Doten) Washburn born 3 September 1767 in Liverpool, Queens, Nova Scotia; daughter of Edward Doten and Phebe (Finney) Doten (married 1738 Plymouth, MA); married a Washburn. Hosea Washburn b. 09 Jun 1765.
findings.claimed_facts: Hannah Doten b. 3 Sep 1767 Liverpool, Queens, NS; parents Edward Doten & Phebe Finney; married Hosea Washburn. Matches worklist birth date/place and parents exactly.
findings.notes: Birth date/place and parents match worklist exactly. Possible death-date conflict: a source gives d. 27 Aug 1864 while worklist says 11 Aug 1864 (worklist death place Avon, Franklin, ME). Spouse Hosea Washburn confirmed.

findings.search_query: John Bridges 1769 New Boston NH Charlotte Washington Maine Anna Hutchings findagrave
findings.source_title: Anna Hutchings - Find a Grave Memorial (and SYNGENEIA John Benjamin Bridges)
findings.snippet: Find a Grave memorial exists for Anna Hutchings (worklist spouse Anna Livingston Hutchings). Genealogy of the John Bridge family in America (1632-1924) and SYNGENEIA reference a John Bridges line; New Boston, NH and Charlotte, Washington Co., ME locations appear in associated records.
findings.claimed_facts: Spouse Anna Hutchings has a Find a Grave memorial; John Bridges line documented in published Bridge family genealogy. Could not extract John's exact birth/death from a single record (Find a Grave returned 403 to fetch).
findings.notes: Partial match: spouse memorial exists, surname/place plausible. Did not confirm John Bridges b.1769 / d.1865 directly; Find a Grave page blocked automated fetch. Needs manual lookup of the Anna Hutchings memorial and linked spouse.

findings.search_query: Lydia Whitney 1743 Groton Massachusetts Stephen Pierce Joseph Whitney Abigail Nutting
findings.source_title: Lydia Whitney & Groton MA Vital Records (Whitney Research Group)
findings.snippet: Lydia, daughter of Joseph and Abigail [Nutting] Whitney, born August 18, 1743 in Groton, Massachusetts. Lydia Whitney (1743- ) married Stephen Pierce.
findings.claimed_facts: Born 18 Aug 1743 Groton, MA; parents Joseph Whitney & Abigail Nutting; married Stephen Pierce. Matches worklist birth date, parents, and spouse exactly.
findings.notes: Birth date, parents, and spouse all match worklist. Death (1806 Groton) not independently confirmed in the records seen. WRG is a well-curated vital-records compilation.

findings.search_query: Nicholas Gilman 1731 Exeter New Hampshire Ann Taylor Daniel Gilman Mary Lord
findings.source_title: Nicholas Gilman (1731-1783) - FamilySearch / Wikipedia
findings.snippet: Nicholas Gilman born 21 October 1731 in Exeter, Rockingham County, New Hampshire to Daniel Gilman (1702-1780) and Mary Lord (1702-1735); died 1783; married Ann Taylor; children John Taylor, Nicholas, Nathaniel. Held a colonelcy in the War for Independence; New Hampshire's first State treasurer.
findings.claimed_facts: Born 21 Oct 1731 Exeter NH; parents Daniel Gilman & Mary Lord; died 1783 Exeter; married Ann Taylor; father of US Constitution signer Nicholas Gilman Jr. and Gov. John Taylor Gilman.
findings.notes: All worklist facts (birth date/place, parents, spouse, death year/place) confirmed; corroborated by Wikipedia and Familypedia. Notable: he was a Revolutionary colonel and NH's first state treasurer.

findings.search_query: Philip Flanders Danforth 1759 Boscawen NH Freedom Maine Mary Tibbets Revolutionary pensioner
findings.source_title: Maine Genealogy Archives: Revolutionary War Pensioners, 1840 / Freedom Cemetery
findings.snippet: Philip Flanders, age 82, listed as a Revolutionary War pensioner residing in Freedom, Maine, in the 1840 federal pension census (head of household Phineas Warren area).
findings.claimed_facts: A Philip Flanders, age 82 in 1840 (=> b. ~1758), Revolutionary War pensioner living in Freedom, Waldo Co., Maine. Matches worklist birth ~1759 and Freedom, ME residence/death.
findings.notes: Name confusion in worklist: recorded as 'Phillip 5Gran Danforth' with father 'Philip Flanders5 Grandfather' — the subject's actual surname appears to be FLANDERS, not Danforth. 1840 pensioner age 82 in Freedom ME strongly fits a 1758/59 birth. Boscawen NH origin plausible (Flanders family of Boscawen documented). Flag surname Flanders vs Danforth for review.

findings.search_query: Priscilla Noble 1785 St Stephen New Brunswick James Sprague Machiasport John Noble Jemima Purdy
findings.source_title: Priscilla (Noble) Sprague (1785-aft.1860) | WikiTree
findings.snippet: Priscilla (Noble) Sprague born 5 July 1785 in New Brunswick, Canada; parents John Noble (1762-1843) and Jemima (Purdy) Noble (1765-1859); married James Sprague (1777-bef.1860). James and Priscilla of Charlotte sold and moved to Machiasport in 1845.
findings.claimed_facts: Born 5 Jul 1785 (St Stephen, Charlotte Co., NB); parents John Noble & Jemima Purdy; married James Sprague; moved to Machiasport, Washington Co., ME 1845. Living after 1860.
findings.notes: Birth date, parents (John Noble & Jemima Purdy), and Machiasport all match worklist. NOTE: WikiTree gives husband James Sprague b.1777 d.bef.1860 — this is DIFFERENT from worklist person #36 James Sprague (b.1746 d.1799). The husband here matches worklist spouse label 'James Sprague** Jr'. Flag spouse identity for review.

findings.search_query: Sarah Sedgley 1766 Bowdoinham Maine Jonathan Fairfield
findings.source_title: Family Group Sheet: Jonathan Fairfield / Sarah Sedgley (Albion, Maine Genealogy F2719)
findings.snippet: Sarah Sedgley, died 20 Jan 1824, buried China Village Cemetery, China, ME. Husband Jonathan Fairfield (b. c.1763, d. 20 Apr 1843, buried China Village Cem). He had a prior marriage to Eunice Crosby Fessenden.
findings.claimed_facts: Married Jonathan Fairfield; died 20 Jan 1824; buried China Village Cemetery, China, Kennebec Co., ME. Husband Jonathan Fairfield d.1843.
findings.notes: Death date 20 Jan 1824 matches worklist EXACTLY; spouse Jonathan Fairfield confirmed; burial China (Kennebec Co.) consistent with worklist death place 'Kennebec County, Maine'. Birth 1766 Bowdoinham and father Robert Sedgley not shown on this sheet.
